title: Link Information
breadcrumbs: Integrate a User-Defined Database Connector with Uniface
text: Link Information On some operating systems, the installation program asks for a link information line. The installation program incorporates this line in the link command files that link Uniface applications or the Uniface Server. The link information line is specific to your operating system. Refer to the documentation for your operating system and linker for more information about conventions, syntax, and so on. With most operating systems, the link command file consists of a link command followed by a list of object files and object libraries. The text that you specify as the link information line is inserted in the list at the appropriate place. On the link information line you must specify the following: The object file or files, or library containing the DBMS connector The object libraries that are required to link with the API of the DBMS You do not need to specify the standard operating system object libraries, because either the Uniface installation program inserts these in the link command file, or the linker uses them automatically. You can use the link command file generated by the installation program to construct link command files or ‘make files’ in your development environment. Full Path Specification The object files and libraries on the link information line must be preceded by a full directory path, or by an environment variable or logical specifying the path. You must define the environment variables or logicals whenever you link a Uniface application or Uniface Server that uses the user-defined connector. It is a good idea to define these environment variables or logicals in a systemwide logon command file. For more information, consult your operating system documentation. Was this topic helpful?
